# Competitive Analysis — Project Management Tools for Product Teams

## Competitors Analyzed

### 1. Asana
- **Strengths:** Clean UI, excellent mobile app, great integrations ecosystem, generous free tier
- **Weaknesses:** Limited customization, poor reporting, no built-in time tracking
- **Onboarding:** Simple 3-step wizard, sample project pre-loaded, skip-friendly
- **Pricing:** Free for up to 15 users, Premium $10.99/user/month

### 2. Linear
- **Strengths:** Developer-focused, blazing fast, keyboard-first, beautiful design
- **Weaknesses:** Limited for non-engineering workflows, no portfolio view, small integration library
- **Onboarding:** Minimal — create workspace, import from Jira/GitHub, start immediately
- **Pricing:** Free for up to 250 issues, Plus $8/user/month

### 3. Notion
- **Strengths:** Flexible (docs + tasks + wikis), great content creation, AI features
- **Weaknesses:** Slow with large datasets, PM features are basic, steep learning curve
- **Onboarding:** Template gallery to start, gradual feature discovery, community templates
- **Pricing:** Free for individuals, Plus $8/user/month

### 4. Monday.com
- **Strengths:** Visual and colorful, excellent for non-technical users, strong automations
- **Weaknesses:** Can feel cluttered, pricing is high, feature bloat
- **Onboarding:** Industry-specific templates, interactive tutorial, 14-day trial
- **Pricing:** Basic $9/user/month (minimum 3 users)

## Key Findings

### Phone Verification
- Asana: Email only ✅
- Linear: Email + optional 2FA ✅
- Notion: Email or Google SSO ✅
- Monday.com: Email only ✅
- **Our product: Phone mandatory ❌ — We are the only one requiring phone verification.**

### Time to First Task
- Linear: ~1 minute (keyboard shortcuts, minimal setup)
- Asana: ~3 minutes (guided wizard with sample project)
- Notion: ~5 minutes (template selection + customization)
- Monday.com: ~4 minutes (template-based)
- **Our product: ~10+ minutes (mandatory wizard, phone verify, no sample data)**

### Progressive Disclosure
- Linear: Excellent — features appear as needed
- Asana: Good — basic view by default, advanced in settings
- Notion: Moderate — flexible but overwhelming initially
- Monday.com: Poor — too many visible options
- **Our product: Poor — all features visible from start**
